name: Emirates
philosophy: An inspired interpretation of Emirates — the Dubai-headquartered flag carrier whose brand identity is the cinematic A380 first-class experience translated into web language. Canvas is warm cream or deep desert-night-black. The signature palette is the Emirates red (#d71920) and gold trim. Type runs a custom Arabic-Latin co-typesetting system — the brand often shows Arabic naskh and Latin grotesk side-by-side at matched scale, a quiet declaration of the UAE origin. Photography is cinematic luxury aviation: A380 cabin interiors with onboard showers, first-class suites with sliding doors, business-class bars at 40,000 feet. The brand voice is 'fly better than business class' — gold trim, marble lavatories, chef-prepared meals — never apologetic about the luxury.

design_language.motion_signature: Cinematic A380 cabin footage as hero — slow tracking shots through first-class suites, champagne pours, lounge bars at altitude. Cross-fades at 1.5s — slower than budget carriers, faster than ultra-luxury hotels. Hover lifts product tiles 4px with a soft shadow.

trademark_signals: Emirates red (#d71920) on the wordmark and on the primary CTA — the iconic logotype with the 'wing' calligraphic flourish
Gold accent (#c9a55c) on first-class and business-class imagery — trim lines on seat-frame photography, on tier-tab dividers, on the wordmark sub-line
Arabic-Latin co-typesetting — destination names appearing in both Arabic naskh and Latin grotesk on hero pages, refusing English-only globalization
A380 cabin photography as primary visual — onboard showers, first-class suites with sliding doors, the lounge bar at altitude — cinematic luxury aviation imagery
Globe-with-routes hero device — the Emirates network shown as a world-globe with red flight arcs radiating from Dubai (DXB) — the brand IS the Dubai hub
ai_slop_avoided: Don't drop the Arabic typography on global pages. The bilingual presence is the brand's UAE-origin declaration; English-only reads as deracinated
Don't substitute Emirates red with a brighter or pinker variant. The red is locked at #d71920 — any shift is off-brand
Don't show economy-class as primary imagery. The brand's voltage is first-class and business-class luxury; leading with economy reads as off-brand budget-carrier
Don't strip the gold trim. The gold-on-red palette is the recognized Emirates lockup; using only red reads as 'generic airline brand'
Don't render the route network as a flat map. The brand uses a 3D globe with radiating arcs from Dubai — flat 2D maps lose the 'Dubai is the hub' message
